_doc: Regression-gate baseline for evals/gate.mjs. These are the COMMITTED, deterministic targets — graded with no LLM, so they can run in CI on every push that touches the skill/MCP. Thresholds were set from the measured baseline on 2026-06-20 (the live .harness/ demo graded 6/6 after the #eef2f0 tokenization fix; the bad fixture graded 1/6). A5 (impeccable detect) is enforced only when the detector is available (local); on a CI runner without ~/.claude/skills/impeccable it is reported as skipped, never a failure — so A1-A4 are the deterministic CI floor. The builder-loop briefs (train/heldout) are graded separately via `bun evals/gate.mjs --suite <built-dir>`; they need an LLM to build, so they are NOT part of the per-push CI gate.

targets.note: The seeded Helix demo users receive on install — the real shipping artifact. Must stay a clean pass.
Deliberately bad. Guards the grader against silently going permissive — if a change makes grade.mjs pass everything, this target turns red.

suite._doc: For --suite runs (the LLM builder-loop arm). Aggregate floor across all built briefs and the parity floor: the WORST single design-language must not drop below worstFloor. Set from the eval baseline; raise as the loop improves.

handoff._doc: For --handoff runs: how complete the structured sections (spec/dataModel/api) are for subagent-driven-development. The shipped demo should be a complete handoff example.
